epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Wheals/angioedema for >6 weeks; identify triggers and exclude urticarial vasculitis; basic labs guided by history; align with EAACI/GA2LEN/EuroGuiDerm guidance

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; refractory cases need specialist access and biologics

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care often treats with antihistamines; specialist referral for refractory cases
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Second-generation antihistamines (up-dosing), short corticosteroid bursts for flares; omalizumab or cyclosporine for refractory CSU in specialist care
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to omalizumab and specialist allergy/dermatology services in SUS
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Antihistamines widely available and clear stepwise guideline frameworks
